text: This place has a great selection of merchandise. But its service is worse than non-existent. I recently bought two expensive Stressless lounge chairs and ottomans from them. After a lengthy delay they were delivered and the deliverymen assembled them. They came with a tray attached to the armchair, which we discovered after the men left tilted badly. We complained and got bounced around. We were finally told they don't warrant their furniture and I should call the manufacturer, who said Lee's are the ones responsible. This went on for months, after which the manufacturer sent the regional sales manufacturer over to our house and he discovered that the chairs had been improperly assembled. He took them apart and re-assembled them. The tray is now straight, the armrests, which were on backward look much better and everything is fine.  I also had a very bad experience with them in 2009 regarding some expensive lighting fixtures I bought from them. I guess I should have stopped buying from them then, but they have such nice products that I was stupid enough to go back,
name: Lee's Art Shop
category: Art supply store||Art gallery||Craft store||Furniture store||Gift shop||Lighting store||Picture frame shop||Stationery store

text: My wife is an artist, so sometimes I pick up art supplies for her. I went to Lee's to purchase some markers, but the clueless staff directed me to the wrong ones. Not really knowing the difference, I purchased them, only to fins out when I got home they were the wrong kind. I tried to return them the next day, explaining my mistake, but the staff just stonewalled me saying they do not accept marker returns - they even insinuated that I had taken them home, used them, and was trying to return them now that I was done.  From now on, I'm going to shop online, or go downtown to Blick's or AI Friedman, or someplace that's actually willing to work with a customer to resolve a dispute.
name: Lee's Art Shop
category: Art supply store||Art gallery||Craft store||Furniture store||Gift shop||Lighting store||Picture frame shop||Stationery store

text: We arrived and asked how much it would be to park and leave by Saturday morning at 10:00 am. We asked this clear as day, several times, and confirmed several times. Jose told us very plainly that to leave at that time on Saturday would be $82. When we arrived at that time on Saturday, he told us the price was now $164, and that we had misunderstood him. We hadn't. We asked to speak to his boss and insisted that he was the boss, that we had misunderstood, and that we had to pay $164. Just be aware this place will mislead you and ream you if it gets a chance.
name: Icon Parking
category: Parking garage||Parking lot
